data.name: CRISPR Gene Drives
data.description: Self-propagating genetic modifications that spread through wild populations. Can potentially eliminate malaria-carrying mosquitoes or invasive species.
data.organization: Target Malaria, UC San Diego (Esvelt lab), Imperial College
data.status: Lab demonstrated; field trials pending; regulatory and ethical concerns significant
data.breakthrough: Target Malaria's gene drive mosquitoes reduced population 90%+ in lab; first confined field study in Burkina Faso

data.name: Cell-Free Protein Synthesis
data.description: Producing proteins without living cells, using cell extracts containing ribosomes and enzymes. Enables rapid prototyping and production of toxic or difficult proteins.
data.organization: Sutro Biopharma, Tierra Biosciences, Cell-Free Tech
data.status: Commercial; Sutro's ovarian cancer drug (luvelta) in Phase 3
data.breakthrough: CFPS enables 24-hour design-to-protein cycles vs weeks with cell-based systems

data.name: Minimal Synthetic Cell (JCVI-syn3.0)
data.description: A bacterium with the smallest known genome (473 genes) that can self-replicate. Built by systematically removing genes from Mycoplasma mycoides to identify essential functions.
data.status: 2016 milestone; syn3A (493 genes) with corrected division published 2021
data.breakthrough: Defined the minimal gene set for cellular life; foundation for custom synthetic organisms

data.name: Xenobots / Anthrobots
data.description: Self-assembling biological robots made from frog (Xenobots) or human (Anthrobots) cells. Can move, heal, and carry payloads without genetic modification.
data.organization: Tufts University (Michael Levin), University of Vermont
data.status: Lab demonstrated; Xenobot 3.0 (2021) can self-replicate; Anthrobots (2023) from human lung cells
data.breakthrough: First living, self-replicating robots; Anthrobots can heal damaged neural tissue in vitro

data.name: Precision Fermentation
data.description: Using engineered microbes to produce specific food proteins, fats, and molecules identical to animal-derived versions. Can replace animal agriculture.
data.organization: Perfect Day (dairy), Motif FoodWorks, Geltor, EVERY Company
data.status: Commercial; Perfect Day's animal-free whey protein in products since 2020
data.breakthrough: Animal-identical dairy proteins from engineered yeast at competitive costs

data.name: DNA Data Storage
data.description: Encoding digital data in synthetic DNA molecules. Theoretical density: 215 petabytes per gram. Potential solution for long-term archival storage.
data.organization: Microsoft/UW (Molecular Information Systems Lab), Catalog, Iridia
data.status: Demonstrated; Microsoft stored 200MB in DNA (2019); read/write speed is bottleneck
data.breakthrough: Automated DNA storage system demonstrated (2021); Iridia developing chip-scale DNA writer

data.name: Engineered Living Materials
data.description: Materials made from living cells that can grow, self-repair, and respond to environmental stimuli. Applications in construction, medicine, and sensing.
data.organization: MIT (Lu lab), Imperial College, Wyss Institute
data.status: Research; self-healing concrete (Basilisk) commercial; living sensors in development
data.breakthrough: Bacteria-based self-healing concrete reduces crack formation by 80%

data.name: Metabolic Engineering for Chemicals
data.description: Engineering microbes to produce chemicals, fuels, and materials from sugar or CO₂ feedstocks, replacing petroleum-derived processes.
data.organization: Ginkgo Bioworks, Zymergen (now Ginkgo), LanzaTech, Genomatica
data.status: Commercial; LanzaTech converts industrial waste gas to ethanol; Genomatica produces bio-BDO at scale
data.breakthrough: LanzaTech's gas fermentation plants operating in China, producing fuel-grade ethanol from steel mill waste gas
